item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every Mr on the list has their name changed, because that's what this guy's anomaly is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm admittedly not sure since I'm not familiar with GAW, might be something to contact the author directly about? I feel like it's intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a reason why Mr. Meme is marked as "Mr. Popular" on this page?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I was expecting the "literal" part to be the figurative form of literal, like a serial killer who is so blatantly a serial killer it's comical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm gonna be fair, I read Jude's explanation of this one before I read the SCP itself so I'm biased.

That said, a trans character involved in the creation of an anomaly that forces you to use an unfitting name that you don't like? And the working title being "Mister Empathy?" If this wasn't deliberate then you're one serendipitous motherflipper.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP mentions Gamers Against Weed. I propose linking it to the hub.

Editing this:

Its chest bears a tattoo reading "Mr. Literal Serial Killer, by Gamers Against Weed".

To this:

Its chest bears a tattoo reading "Mr. Literal Serial Killer, by Gamers Against Weed".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: crosses self

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that's actually my name in real life

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
did NOBODY ELSE notice that the site director apparently got caught in the blast

his name is Roland Joints for god's sake

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On the one hand, the absence of a list of name alteration examples is glaring. On the other, such a list would probably be absurd enough to take away from the rest of the article. Odd predicament.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Based on his unfitting name, and his effect, I bet that he's not always been "Mr. Literal Serial Killer". I'd like to imagine that when he messes up he sometimes hits himself (like with a bomb, for example), and his name changes again.

EDIT: This is a very underrated GAW mister, this really deserves more love.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how you didn't actually build Mr. Literal Serial Killer around a pun, but actually did something clever with it. The name bomb at the end was a nice twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Starting a sentence with "Just" implies the exclusion of alternatives.

Regardless, something about threatening an SCP with amputation rubs me the wrong way, and I don't see a strong justification for its inclusion, so I'm not going to change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, no one said restraints have to go along with the fingers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Leaving him unrestrained increases the odds that he does it again, which nobody at that site really wants — "can't" is better than "won't", which is why death threats are rarely incorporated into containment procedures. The only person who would benefit here is 2586, who nobody involved is particularly sympathetic towards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Recommending 24/7 restraint of SCP-2586

Just threaten him with amputation. Next time he mimics something as much as opening a pocket knife, have his fingers cut off. Can't mimic something if there are no extremities to mimic with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any ideas on how I could touch up the description?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bit shocked to see it is rated relatively low among GAW's Misters. I think that this shows what GAW is as an GoI and a reminder of GAW's pacifism and subversion of any conventions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some tonal issues in the description but I liked the effect enough to ignore them, and the bomb bit enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really digging all the different interpretations people have come up with for this one. I always wanted an SCP that has more to it than what I intended, and I guess I got it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought by using their weapon on somebody, Mr. Literal Serial Killer was basically killing them by making them not them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would've preferred the Misters document be after the other addendum. But +1 anyway.

I'm just now imagining, like, this guy walking around thinking he's a hit-man, getting a name for his target and then assuming he's succeeded at his mission because he took out the name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm all for GAW doing well and all, but this is just fucking stupid. I get that the name is supposed to be ironic, but it just paints a horrendously bad image of GAW's thoughts leading up to his creation - "what if x but not?"

The other weedsters have at least a mildly intriguing story or twist on them. This has nothing but aggressively unhumourous irony, and is no better than the bad ideas that were trying to be ousted. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it works because the SCP seems to believe that he is a serial killer even though he's not actually killing anyone. The name is supposed to be ironic in that he's not a literal serial killer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The effect didn't do much for me, but the bit about the bomb was clever enough for an upthingy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely nothing. It's an unfitting name for an SCP that gives people unfitting names.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was expecting the non-cereal-related pun. I don't understand what changing someone's name has to do with being a "literal serial killer".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. The use of imaginary weapons and the effect they had was really unique. Also October 19th is my birthday so that's pretty dope.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was an unexpected and interesting interpretation of the name. Much more in the GAW spirit than someone who was literally a serial killer. The first sentence of the containment set up an expectation that was dismantled effectively.

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded: THE CEREAL IS PEOPLE! THE CEREAL IS PEOPLLEEEE!!!

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded:
Making a pun out of him being a killer of serial

Huh, I think you could get some milage out this with the popular perception that if you like something it is going to be canceled early. Seems like a GAW kind of idea, to me, given that people complain a lot on the Internet about it.

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded:
Ahhh. Yeah, that's just a gimmick for this article, it'd lose meaning if I changed all the other misters to match it.
Me=not very clear.

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded:
My mistake. I thought you were going to change all the names on the other lists as well.
Me=not very bright.

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded: Adding a link to this SCP. The same changes that have been made for every other Mister that's been posted.

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded: Not that part, that's fine. I'm talking about changing the other lists. What are these planned changes?

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry for being averse to doing work that I might have to undo later?

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded:
I haven't updated any of the other Misters Against Weed cuz I wanna be sure this'll survive first.

I'm not sure I like where this is going.

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded: Neil Gaiman did a great creepy story going the other way. A convention of cereal collectors is actually serial Killers. The convention discussion topics are fantastic.

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded: Then it would be both Mr. Just Has The Tattoo and Mr. Literal Serial Killer. Two for the price of one!

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded: I would imagine trying to create a article about a "cereal killer" would be next to impossible, considering how many times I've heard that joke before.

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded: I'd assume at least one "put a tattoo on an existing serial killer".

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded:
Making a pun out of him being a killer of serials (i.e. particular works of fiction) or being a killer of cereal. Pretty much everything I saw was a variant of one of those two ideas, iirc. The problem, of course, being that puns can only rarely form the backbone of an article, and in-universe there would be literally no reason to make the mister except for the pun, which is lame.

I changed the list on this article since the whole theme is misnaming things. I haven't updated any of the other Misters Against Weed cuz I wanna be sure this'll survive first.

item.title: Re: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded:
What are some examples of some "bad" mr. Literal serial killer?
Also, did you change the list?

item.title: coldpost theater
item.content:encoded:
the hardest part of this was coming up with inaccurate misters names without like, forcing any particular interpretation of them

everybody else's ideas for mr. literal serial killer were bad so i wrote mine instead

image is from here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Richard_Murphy_mug_shot.jpg it's public domain
